If we're discussing the plant Helichrysum, here's a paragraph about it:

 Helichrysum is a genus of flowering plants in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the everlasting flower, due to its ability to dry well without wilting. The health benefits of Helichrysum include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in wound healing, reducing pain and inflammation, and protecting against cell damage. The bioactive constituents of Helichrysum include sesquiterpene lactones, such as dihydro-artemisinin and dihydro-artemisinol, and volatile oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Helichrysum is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which are used to treat various health conditions, including arthritis, digestive issues, and skin problems.
